prompt: I want to run two Claude sessions at the same time — one working on a new auth feature and one fixing a bug in the API. How do I set that up without them conflicting?

assertions.text: Response includes a structured agent brief with at least 3 of: working directory path, branch name, what to do, what NOT to touch
assertions.criteria: The response provides a briefing template or example that includes at least three of the following for each agent session: (1) the absolute working directory path, (2) the branch name, (3) what the agent should work on, (4) what the agent should NOT modify or touch

assertions.text: Response explicitly tells each agent what NOT to touch (negative scope)
assertions.criteria: The response explicitly includes guidance on what each agent should NOT do or NOT modify — not just what it should do. For example, 'do not touch packages/ui' or 'do not modify routes.ts'

assertions.text: Response recommends opening draft PRs for in-progress parallel sessions
assertions.criteria: The response recommends using draft PRs (GitHub draft pull requests) while work is still in progress in the worktrees, as a way to make parallel work visible

assertions.text: Response addresses how to merge the two PRs (one at a time, rebase the other after)
assertions.criteria: The response explains that PRs should be merged one at a time, and that after the first merges, the second session should rebase on main before it can be merged
